title.en: May I pass through? (Navigating crowded places)

description.en: Stuck in a crowd? Learn how to clear your path politely using "잠시만요" and other essential phrases for public spaces.
description.ko: 지하철이나 좁은 길에서 사람들을 지나가야 할 때! 무례하지 않게 길을 터달라고 요청하는 에티켓과 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Public. Subway. Manners. Request. Navigation. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 사람이 많은 곳에서 당황한 목소리로 바로 시작
content.text: Mina, help! I was on the subway this morning, and it was so crowded that I almost missed my stop!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I kept saying "실례합니다" (Sil-rye-ham-ni-da), but nobody seemed to move. Did I say something wrong?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "실례합니다" 는 틀린 말은 아니지만 사실 조금 너무 정중하게 들릴 수 있어요.
content.translation: Ah, "Sil-rye-ham-ni-da" isn't wrong, but it can actually sound a bit too formal.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 지하철이나 좁은 길에서는 훨씬 더 빠르고 효과적인 마법의 단어가 있거든요.
content.translation: In subways or narrow paths, there's a much faster and more effective 'magic word'.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Really? Better than "Excuse me"? What is it?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 바로! "잠시만요" 혹은..., "잠시만 지나갈게요" 예요.
content.translation: It’s "Jam-si-man-yo" or "Jam-si-man ji-na-gal-ge-yo."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 복잡한 공공장소에서 아주 자연스럽게 길을 터달라고 요청하는 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn reactions to naturally ask people to clear the path in crowded public places!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 잠시만요. This sounds so short! Is it really enough?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! "잠시만 기다려 달라" 또는..., "잠시만 길을 비켜달라" 라는 뜻이 모두 담겨 있어서 정말 편리해요.
content.translation: Yes! It contains meanings like "Please wait a moment" or "Please clear the path for a moment," so it's very convenient.

insight.title: 잠시만요
insight.usage_tip: '잠시(A moment)' + '만(Only)' + '요(Polite ending)'. This is the most versatile phrase to get someone's attention or pass through a crowd.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 잠시만요! 잠시만 지나갈게요!
content.translation: Excuse me! I'll pass through for a moment!

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to be a bit more specific and polite at the same time?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "잠시만 지나가도 될까요?" 라고 물어보세요.
content.translation: In that case, ask, "Jam-si-man ji-na-ga-do doel-kka-yo?"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 허락을 구하는 방식이라 훨씬 더 부드러운 느낌을 줘요.
content.translation: Since it's asking for permission, it gives a much softer feeling.

insight.title: 잠시만 지나가도 될까요?
insight.description: 'May I pass through for a second?'
insight.usage_tip: '잠시(A moment)' + '지나가다(To pass by)' + '도 될까요?(May I/Is it okay?)'. Use this when you need to move past someone who is standing still, like in a cinema or theater aisle.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm on a very crowded subway line number 2, and the doors are about to open at my station!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저기요! 잠시만요! 저 이번에 내려야 해요.
content.translation: Excuse me, just a moment! I have to get off this time.

insight.title: 이번에 내려야 해요
insight.description: 'I have to get off this time'
insight.usage_tip: Giving a clear reason (getting off) makes people move out of your way much faster.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아! 네! 죄송합니다.
content.translation: Ah, yes! I'm sorry.

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 길을 비켜준 사람에게 살짝 고개를 숙이며
content.text: 감사합니다. 잠시만 지나갈게요!
content.translation: Thank you. I'll just pass through!

insight.title: 잠시만 지나갈게요
insight.usage_tip: Using the '-ㄹ게요' ending shows your intention clearly and politely.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네, 이쪽으로 지나가세요!
content.translation: Yes, please pass through this way!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 휴~ 다행이다. 감사합니다!
content.translation: Phew, thank you! I'm glad I made it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, once I said "잠시만요," it was like the Red Sea parting! Everyone let me through.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국에서는 짧고 명확하게 내 의사를 표현하는 리액션이 복잡한 곳에서 아주 유용하답니다.
content.translation: Right. In Korea, reactions that express your intention clearly and briefly are very useful in crowded places.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "public manners" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 잠시만요!
content.translation: Just a moment! / Excuse me!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 잠시만 지나갈게요!
content.translation: I'll just pass through!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 잠시만 지나가도 될까요?
content.translation: May I pass through for a second?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이번에 내려야 해요!
content.translation: I have to get off this time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 길 좀 비켜주시겠어요?
content.translation: Could you clear the path a bit?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 복잡한 지하철이나 길거리에서 당황하지 말고, 웃으면서 말해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, don't panic in a crowded subway or street today; try saying it with a smile.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "잠시만 지나갈게요!"
content.translation: I'll just pass through!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 잠시 후에 다시 연락드릴게요. (I'll contact you again in a short while.)

vocabulary.meaning: To pass by / To go through
vocabulary.example: 좁은 골목을 차가 지나가고 있어요. (A car is passing through a narrow alley.)

vocabulary.meaning: To move aside / To clear the way
vocabulary.example: 길을 비켜주셔서 정말 감사합니다. (Thank you so much for clearing the way.)
소음이 조금 발생할 수 있으니 양해 부탁드립니다. (Please understand as there might be some noise.)
주말이라 쇼핑몰이 정말 복잡하네요. (The shopping mall is really crowded since it's the weekend.)

vocabulary.meaning: To open up / To clear (a path)
vocabulary.example: 사람들이 다친 사람을 위해 길을 터주었어요. (People cleared the path for the injured person.)
